title: Replacing a sofa cushion with new foam
subtitle: Measure, cut, wrap, and refit a sagging seat cushion
excerpt: How to replace a worn-out sofa seat cushion insert with new CMHR foam: measuring the cover, ordering cut-to-size foam, wrapping with Dacron, and fitting the new insert into the existing cover.
sourceNotes: The Upholsterer's Step-by-Step Handbook (Dorling Kindersley). Collins Complete DIY Manual.

content.text: A sofa cushion that sags and does not return to shape has lost its foam resilience. The cover is usually in good condition; the insert is not. Replacing only the foam insert costs a fraction of a new cushion.
is cut to size by most foam suppliers from a template measurement and arrives ready to fit.

Remove the cushion cover zip and take out the old insert. Measure the interior of the cover: width, depth (front to back), and height. The foam must be cut 10 to 20 mm larger than the interior measurement in each direction: the cover should be slightly overfilled to hold its shape.
Step 2, order or cut the foam

Order cut-to-size CMHR foam in medium or firm grade from a foam supplier, providing the overfilled dimensions. For a seat cushion, 100 to 125 mm is a typical height; firm density holds shape better under daily use. If cutting yourself, use a sharp utility knife with long strokes rather than short sawing cuts; a bread knife also gives a clean cut on foam.

large enough to wrap around the foam and overlap underneath by 50 mm. Wrap firmly, pulling the Dacron taut without compressing the foam, and hold the overlap in place with a few strips of tape. The Dacron fills the cover fully and prevents the foam corners from poking through the fabric.

Open the cover zip fully. Feed the wrapped foam into the cover, working one corner in first, then the other. If the foam is correctly oversized, it will require some compression to fit: this is correct. Pull the cover firmly over all four corners, smooth the top face, and close the zip. Place the cushion in the sofa and check it sits evenly.

attrs.fix: Add a second layer of Dacron around the existing foam before refitting, which will fill the cover more fully.
attrs.cause: Foam was not oversized sufficiently, or the Dacron wrap is too thin.
attrs.symptom: Cushion still looks flat or the corners are rounded rather than square

attrs.fix: Remove and trim the foam by 10 mm in the height and depth. The cover should require firm compression to close, not wrenching.
attrs.cause: Foam cut too large in all dimensions.
attrs.symptom: Foam too stiff to zip the cover closed
